Title: Engineering of nicotianamine synthesis enhances cadmium mobility in plants and results in higher seed cadmium concentrations

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotianamine-related Cd and Zn homeostasis traits
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / mineral nutrient and toxic metal homeostasis trait
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/seedlings; indirect effects on seeds discussed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inferred from mutant phenotypes in seedlings
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NAS4 causes Cd hypersensitivity and contributes strongly to Zn detoxification phenotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and triple mutant root growth assays under Cd and Zn excess
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among single mutants, nas4 was significantly more Cd sensitive than Col-0. 2. Triple mutants lacking NAS4 showed Cd hypersensitivity, whereas nas1nas2nas3 resembled wild type. 3. NAS4 also contributes to Zn detoxification, especially together with NAS2.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although NAS4 is a pathway enzyme gene, it was not the transgene primary focus; it was functionally resolved through mutant analysis as a core endogenous contributor.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Endogenous NAS4 status may influence Cd/Zn tolerance and should be considered when engineering nicotianamine-related biofortification.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces tolerance to Cd and Zn.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native locus in mutants; UBQ10 in overexpression constructs for AtNAS2 transgene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotianamine-related Zn and Cd homeostasis traits
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator / mineral nutrient and toxic metal homeostasis trait
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nicotianamine-dependent metal detoxification and mobility
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedlings and seeds via altered NA status
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Endogenous NAS2 contributes mainly to Zn detoxification, while AtNAS2 overexpression drives NA overaccumulation and increased seed Fe, Zn, and Cd
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant analysis; transgenic overexpression; HPLC NA quantification; ICP-OES elemental analysis; root growth assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Zn excess; Cd excess; soil Cd contamination
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Triple mutants indicate NAS2 contributes to Zn detoxification together with NAS4. 2. Overexpressed AtNAS2 strongly elevates nicotianamine levels. 3. Elevated NAS2 activity improves Cd tolerance yet increases seed Cd along with Fe and Zn.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This entry captures endogenous NAS2 contribution distinct from the AtNAS2 transgene-focused pathway role.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for Fe/Zn biofortification, but must be balanced against Cd co-accumulation risk.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA overproduction can elevate toxic Cd in seeds.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter (constitutive); nmt1 promoter in Schizosaccharomyces pombe; GAL1 promoter in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nicotianamine biosynthesis / metal chelator biosynthesis / metal homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Nicotianamine synthesis module for intracellular and long-distance metal chelation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step; direct synthesis of nicotianamine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses three molecules of S-adenosyl-L-methionine to form nicotianamine, a non-proteinogenic metal chelator
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the target metabolite and thereby enhances mobility/chelation of Cd, Fe, and Zn
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Nicotianamine; seed Fe; seed Zn; seed Cd
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Metal chelator; mineral nutrient / toxic metal-related trait
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nicotianamine chelates transition metals and supports metal homeostasis, long-distance transport, and micronutrient bioavailability; however, it can also mobilize toxic Cd
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole seedlings and leaves for nicotianamine; seeds for Fe, Zn, and Cd
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene expressed constitutively in Arabidopsis overexpression lines; induced in yeast under promoter control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases nicotianamine accumulation, improves Cd, Zn, and Ni tolerance, increases seed Fe and Zn, and unintentionally increases seed Cd accumulation under environmentally relevant Cd exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and yeast; mutant complementation; metabolite quantification by HPLC; elemental profiling by ICP-OES; root growth assays; Cd uptake assays; confocal localization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for nicotianamine level; not explicitly termed rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cd exposure; Zn excess; Ni excess; soil with environmentally relevant Cd contamination; hydroponic and plate culture metal treatments
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNAS2 expression in yeast causes high nicotianamine accumulation, enhanced Cd uptake/accumulation, and Cd hypersensitivity. 2. In Arabidopsis, mCherry-AtNAS2 complements nicotianamine deficiency and raises nicotianamine levels 5–8-fold. 3. AtNAS2 overexpression improves Cd tolerance but also elevates seed Fe, Zn, and Cd concentrations, revealing a biofortification-associated food safety risk.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper supports formation of intracellular Cd-NA complexes indirectly through competition and uptake data; direct in vivo complex detection was not performed. The same gene was studied in both heterologous yeast systems and Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for Fe/Zn biofortification by increasing nicotianamine, but deployment should be paired with monitoring phytoavailable Cd and ideally combined with low-Cd germplasm or Cd-uptake-reducing alleles to avoid food safety risk.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased seed cadmium accumulation under relevant soil Cd conditions despite improved Fe/Zn biofortification and metal tolerance.
